title: werauhia pycnantha
intro: Werauhia pycnantha, also known as the golden puka or golden kuka, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, native to the Pacific Islands, specifically in New Zealand and the Chatham Islands. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Werauhia pycnantha include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its extract is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
